goals.title: AI Team goal-mode full architecture refactor for /Users/swcrbt/develo...
goals.objective: AI Team goal-mode full architecture refactor for /Users/swcrbt/develop/github/swcrbt/ai-team. Do not downgrade scope. Preserve business behavior and compatibility while replacing fake part-based boundaries with real Dart libraries, splitting core domain/model/orchestration/application/ui/test modules. Subgoals: G1 real Dart library boundaries; G2 core domain/model gateway; G3 orchestration service boundaries; G4 AppController facade with delegated components; G5 focused UI widgets/presenters; G6 tests/docs/final quality gate. Verification after each story, phase commits, final flutter analyze/test/build, git diff --check, clarity cleanup, code review, final commit, then mark aggregate Codex goal complete.
goals.blockedReason: The old story cannot be truthfully checkpointed complete because its Codex objective mismatched the durable pointer objective; recovery subgoals replace it without deleting audit history.
goals.steeringEvidence: Previous ledger records objective-mismatch blocker after code commits; recovery run uses a new aligned Codex aggregate goal.
goals.steeringRationale: The old story cannot be truthfully checkpointed complete because its Codex objective mismatched the durable pointer objective; recovery subgoals replace it without deleting audit history.

goals.title: Recover aligned architecture goal state
goals.objective: Recover the AI Team architecture refactor goal state by replacing the objective-mismatched blocked story with aligned recovery stories, verify the active Codex aggregate goal matches goals.json, and preserve the old blocker evidence in ledger history.
goals.evidence: Recovered durable goal bookkeeping: old G001 preserved as objective-mismatch blocked evidence; G002 started under aligned aggregate objective; get_goal active objective now matches .omx/ultragoal/goals.json codexObjective; omx ultragoal complete-goals prints the same aggregate payload.

goals.title: Audit refactor baseline and blockers
goals.objective: Audit commits e2607d4 through a40d7fe and the current architecture against the original G1-G6 requirements; run baseline flutter analyze, flutter test, and git diff --check; record concrete blocker files and decide which remaining large modules must be refactored instead of accepted as residual risk.
goals.evidence: Baseline audit recorded in docs/architecture_recovery_audit.md. Verified /Users/swcrbt/develop/flutter/bin/flutter analyze passed, /Users/swcrbt/develop/flutter/bin/flutter test passed with 222 tests, and git diff --check passed. Blockers identified: broad AppController, TeamOrchestrator, config_dialogs, domain_test, app_widget_test.

goals.title: Split remaining application responsibilities
goals.objective: Refactor lib/application/app_controller.dart so AppController remains the public facade but delegates configuration validation, conversation/session lifecycle, task queue operations, dispatch lifecycle, and title generation to focused application components with targeted tests under test/application.
goals.evidence: Recovery plan G2 identifies AppController as 1779 lines with mixed facade, validation, queue, session, dispatch, persistence, workspace, and command responsibilities.

goals.title: Refine orchestration boundaries
goals.objective: Refactor remaining TeamOrchestrator responsibilities by extracting secretary summary, assignment recovery, member dispatch, command continuation, and audit/private dispatch policy where doing so reduces mixed workflow logic; preserve serial/parallel assignment, reassignment, private secretary dispatch, command continuation, diagnostics, and audit behavior with focused tests.
goals.evidence: Recovery plan G3 identifies TeamOrchestrator as still near 1000 lines after the first runner extraction.

goals.title: Split dialogs and UI flow tests
goals.objective: Split lib/ui/dialogs/config_dialogs.dart by dialog responsibility and split test/ui/app_widget_test.dart into focused chat rendering, chat scroll, composer, management, dialogs, and orchestration UI tests without reducing coverage or changing user-visible behavior.
goals.evidence: Recovery plan G4 identifies config_dialogs.dart and app_widget_test.dart as large coupled UI files after the first UI split.

goals.title: Split domain tests and compatibility coverage
goals.objective: Split test/core/domain/domain_test.dart into focused domain test files for queued tasks, configuration export, secure persistence, local store paths, role command policy, secretary orchestration domain behavior, and patch proposals while preserving JSON compatibility and behavior coverage.
goals.evidence: Recovery plan G5 identifies domain_test.dart as a 2301-line omnibus test after production domain modules were split.

goals.title: Final gates and durable closeout
goals.objective: Run changed-file cleanup review, full flutter analyze, full flutter test, flutter build macos --debug, git diff --check, independent code review and architecture review; commit the final recovery work; only then complete the active Codex aggregate goal and checkpoint ultragoal with quality-gate evidence.
goals.evidence: Recovery plan G6 requires final verification, review, commit, Codex goal complete, and .omx/ultragoal complete before reporting completion.
